---
title: "28060, san nazzaro sesia, italy"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
import requests
import plotly.graph_objects as go
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from scipy.interpolate import make_interp_spline
from scipy.ndimage import gaussian_filter1d

url = "https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast"
params = {
    "latitude": 41.89,
    "longitude": 12.49,
    "hourly": ["temperature_2m", "relative_humidity_2m", "dew_point_2m", "cloud_cover", "precipitation_probability"],
    "timezone": "auto",
    "forecast_days": 14
}

response = requests.get(url, params=params)
data = response.json()["hourly"]
df = pd.DataFrame(data)
df['time'] = pd.to_datetime(df['time'])

mask = (df['time'].dt.weekday.isin([5, 6])) & (df['time'].dt.hour >= 0) & (df['time'].dt.hour <= 6)
df_wk = df[mask].copy().reset_index(drop=True)

def ultra_smooth(x, y, sigma=1.5):
    y_filtered = gaussian_filter1d(y, sigma=sigma)
    
    x_new = np.linspace(x.min(), x.max(), 500) 
    spline = make_interp_spline(x, y_filtered, k=3)      
    y_smooth = spline(x_new)
    return x_new, y_smooth

x_numeric = np.arange(len(df_wk))
fig = go.Figure()

# Aggiungiamo le tracce
metrics = [
    ('temperature_2m', 'Temp °C', '#377eb8', 1.2),
    ('dew_point_2m', 'Dew Point', '#ff7f00', 1.2),
    ('relative_humidity_2m', 'Humidity %', '#4daf4a', 1.5),
    ('cloud_cover', 'Cloud %', '#f781bf', 2.0),
    ('precipitation_probability', 'Rain %', '#a65628', 1.5)
]

for col, name, color, sig in metrics:
    y_smooth = smooth_data(df_wk[col], sigma=sig)
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=df_wk['time'], 
        y=y_smooth,
        mode='lines',
        name=name,
        line=dict(color=color, width=3, shape='spline'), # 'spline' rende tutto più morbido
        hovertemplate='%{x|%d %b %H:%M}<br>%{y:.1f}'
    ))

# 5. Layout Full Page & Responsive
fig.update_layout(
    margin=dict(l=20, r=20, t=50, b=20),
    height=800,           # Altezza fissa iniziale (si può settare in CSS)
    autosize=True,        # Fondamentale per la responsività
    template="plotly_white",
    xaxis=dict(title="Tempo", tickformat="%d %b %H:%M"),
    yaxis=dict(range=[-5, 105], showticklabels=False),
    legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02, xanchor="right", x=1)
)

fig.show()
```

| variable | legend |
|---|---|
| temperature | <font color="#377eb8">---</font> |
| dew | <font color="#ff7f00">---</font> |
| humidity | <font color="#4daf4a">---</font> |
| cloud | <font color="#f781bf">---</font> |
| precipitation | <font color="#a65628">---</font> |
